volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 1 - Section 113)

sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: τέκνον τυφλοῦ γέροντος Ἀντιγόνη , τίνας
sections.translation: Child of a blind old man , Antigone , to what region have we come , or to what city of men ? Who will entertain the wandering Oedipus today with scanty gifts ?

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: σμικρὸν μὲν ἐξαιτοῦντα , τοῦ σμικροῦ δʼ ἔτι
sections.translation: Little do I crave , and obtain still less than that little , and with that I am content . For patience is the lesson of suffering , and of the long years upon me , and lastly of a noble mind . My child , if you see any resting-place ,

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ἢ πρὸς βεβήλοις ἢ πρὸς ἄλσεσιν θεῶν ,
sections.translation: either on profane ground or by groves of the gods , stop me and set me down , so that we may inquire where we are . We have come to learn as foreigners from the townsmen , and to bring to completion whatever we hear .

sections.label: Section 14
sections.original: πάτερ ταλαίπωρʼ Οἰδίπους , πύργοι μέν , οἳ
sections.translation: Father , toil-worn Oedipus , the towers that

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: πόλιν στέγουσιν , ὡς ἀπʼ ὀμμάτων , πρόσω ·
sections.translation: ring the city , to judge by sight , are far off ; and this place is sacred , to judge from its appearance : laurel , olive , and vine grow thick-set ; and a feathered crowd of nightingales makes music within . So sit here on this unshaped stone ;

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: μακρὰν γὰρ ὡς γέροντι προὐστάλης ὁδόν .
sections.translation: you have travelled a long way for an old man .

sections.label: Section 21
sections.original: κάθιζέ νύν με καὶ φύλασσε τὸν τυφλόν .
sections.translation: Seat me , then , and watch over the blind .

sections.label: Section 22
sections.original: χρόνου μὲν οὕνεκʼ οὐ μαθεῖν με δεῖ τόδε .
sections.translation: If time can teach , I need not learn that .

sections.label: Section 23
sections.original: ἔχεις διδάξαι δή μʼ ὅποι καθέσταμεν ;
sections.translation: Can you tell me , now , where we have arrived ?

sections.label: Section 24
sections.original: τὰς γοῦν Ἀθήνας οἶδα , τὸν δὲ χῶρον οὔ .
sections.translation: Athens I know , but not this place .

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: πᾶς γάρ τις ηὔδα τοῦτό γʼ ἡμὶν ἐμπόρων .
sections.translation: Yes , so much every traveller told us .

sections.label: Section 26
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὅστις ὁ τόπος ἦ μάθω μολοῦσά ποι ;
sections.translation: Well , shall I go and learn what the spot is called ?

sections.label: Section 27
sections.original: ναί , τέκνον , εἴπερ ἐστί γʼ ἐξοικήσιμος .
sections.translation: Yes , child , if indeed it is inhabited .

sections.label: Section 28
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐστὶ μὴν οἰκητός · οἴομαι δὲ δεῖν
sections.translation: It surely is inhabited . But I think there is no need — I see a man nearby .

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: ἦ δεῦρο προσστείχοντα κἀξορμώμενον ;
sections.translation: Setting off and coming toward us ?

sections.label: Section 31
sections.original: καὶ δὴ μὲν οὖν παρόντα · χὤ τί σοι λέγειν
sections.translation: He is at our side already . Speak whatever seems timely to you , for the man is here .

sections.label: Section 33
sections.original: ὦ ξεῖνʼ , ἀκούων τῆσδε τῆς ὑπέρ τʼ ἐμοῦ
sections.translation: Stranger , hearing from this maiden , who has sight both for herself and for me ,

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: σκοπὸς προσήκεις ὧν ἀδηλοῦμεν φράσαι —
sections.translation: that you have arrived as a scout of good fortune for the solving of our doubts —

sections.label: Section 36
sections.original: πρὶν νῦν τὰ πλείονʼ ἱστορεῖν , ἐκ τῆσδʼ ἕδρας
sections.translation: Now , before you question me at length , leave this seat . You occupy ground which is unholy to tread upon .

sections.label: Section 38
sections.original: τίς δʼ ἔσθʼ ὁ χῶρος ; τοῦ θεῶν νομίζεται ;
sections.translation: And what is this ground ? To which of the gods is it sacred ?

sections.label: Section 39
sections.original: ἄθικτος οὐδʼ οἰκητός · αἱ γὰρ ἔμφοβοι
sections.translation: Ground inviolable , on which no one may dwell . The dread

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: θεαί σφʼ ἔχουσι , Γῆς τε καὶ Σκότου κόραι .
sections.translation: goddesses hold it , the daughters of Earth and Darkness .

sections.label: Section 41
sections.original: τίνων τὸ σεμνὸν ὄνομʼ ἂν εὐξαίμην κλύων ;
sections.translation: Who are they ? Whose awful name might I hear and invoke in prayer ?

sections.label: Section 42
sections.original: τὰς πάνθʼ ὁρώσας Εὐμενίδας ὅ γʼ ἐνθάδʼ ἂν
sections.translation: The all-seeing Eumenides the people here would call them : but other names please elsewhere .

sections.label: Section 44
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἵλεῳ μὲν τὸν ἱκέτην δεξαίατο ·
sections.translation: Then graciously may they receive their suppliant !

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: ὡς οὐχ ἕδρας γῆς τῆσδʼ ἂν ἐξέλθοιμʼ ἔτι .
sections.translation: Nevermore will I depart from my seat in this land .

sections.label: Section 46
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐστὶ τοῦτο ;
sections.translation: What does this mean ?

sections.label: Section 46a
sections.original: ξυμφορᾶς ξύνθημʼ ἐμῆς .
sections.translation: The watchword of my fate .

sections.label: Section 47
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδʼ ἐμοί τοι τοὐξανιστάναι πόλεως
sections.translation: I dare not remove you without warrant from the city , until I report what I am doing .

sections.label: Section 48
sections.original: δίχʼ ἐστὶ θάρσος , πρίν γʼ ἂν ἐνδείξω τί δρῶ .
sections.translation: Now by the gods , stranger , do not deny me , hapless wanderer as you see ,

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: τοιόνδʼ ἀλήτην , ὧν σε προστρέπω φράσαι .
sections.translation: the honor of the knowledge for which I beg you .

sections.label: Section 51
sections.original: σήμαινε , κοὐκʼ ἄτιμος ἔκ γʼ ἐμοῦ φανεῖ .
sections.translation: Tell me , and you will not be without honor from me .

sections.label: Section 52
sections.original: τίς ἔσθʼ ὁ χῶρος δῆτʼ , ἐν ᾧ βεβήκαμεν ;
sections.translation: What , then , is the place that we have entered ?

sections.label: Section 53
sections.original: ὅσʼ οἶδα κἀγὼ πάντʼ ἐπιστήσει κλύων ·
sections.translation: All that I myself know , you will hear and learn . This whole place is sacred ;

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: σεμνὸς Ποσειδῶν · ἐν δʼ ὁ πυρφόρος θεὸς
sections.translation: august Poseidon holds it , and in it lives the fire-bearing god , the Titan Prometheus . But as for the spot on which you tread , it is called the bronze threshold of this land , the support of Athens . And the neighboring fields claim Colonus , the horse-rider , for their ancient ruler ;

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: ἀρχηγὸν εἶναι καὶ φέρουσι τοὔνομα
sections.translation: and all the people bear his name in common as their own . Such , you see , stranger , are these haunts . They receive their honor not through story , but rather through our living with them .

sections.label: Section 64
sections.original: ἦ γάρ τινες ναίουσι τούσδε τοὺς τόπους ;
sections.translation: Are there indeed dwellers in this region ?

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: καὶ κάρτα , τοῦδε τοῦ θεοῦ γʼ ἐπώνυμοι .
sections.translation: Yes indeed , the namesakes of that god there Colonus .

sections.label: Section 66
sections.original: ἄρχει τις αὐτῶν ἢ ʼπὶ τῷ πλήθει λόγος ;
sections.translation: Have they a king ? Or does speaking in assembly rest with the masses ?

sections.label: Section 67
sections.original: ἐκ τοῦ κατʼ ἄστυ βασιλέως τάδʼ ἄρχεται .
sections.translation: These parts are ruled by the king in the city .

sections.label: Section 68
sections.original: οὗτος δὲ τίς λόγῳ τε καὶ σθένει κρατεῖ ;
sections.translation: And who is he that is sovereign in counsel and in might ?

sections.label: Section 69
sections.original: Θησεὺς καλεῖται , τοῦ πρὶν Αἰγέως τόκος .
sections.translation: Theseus he is called , son of Aegeus who was before him .

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἄν τις αὐτῷ πομπὸς ἐξ ὑμῶν μόλοι ;
sections.translation: Could a messenger go to him from among you ?

sections.label: Section 71
sections.original: ὡς πρὸς τί λέξων ἢ καταρτύσων μολεῖν ;
sections.translation: With what aim ? To speak , or to prepare his coming ?

sections.label: Section 72
sections.original: ὡς ἂν προσαρκῶν σμικρὰ κερδάνῃ μέγα .
sections.translation: So that by a small service he may find a great gain .

sections.label: Section 73
sections.original: καὶ τίς πρὸς ἀνδρὸς μὴ βλέποντος ἄρκεσις ;
sections.translation: And what help can come from one who cannot see ?

sections.label: Section 74
sections.original: ὅσʼ ἂν λέγωμεν πάνθʼ ὁρῶντα λέξομεν .
sections.translation: In all that I speak there will be vision .

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: οἶσθʼ , ὦ ξένʼ , ὡς νῦν μὴ σφαλῇς ; ἐπείπερ εἰ
sections.translation: Take care now , stranger , that you come to no harm ; for you are noble , if I may judge by your looks , leaving your ill-fortune aside . Stay here , where I found you , until I go and tell these things to the people of this district — not in the city .

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: εἰ χρή σε μίμνειν ἢ πορεύεσθαι πάλιν .
sections.translation: They will decide for you whether you should stay or go back . Stranger exits .

sections.label: Section 81
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , ἦ βέβηκεν ἡμὶν ὁ ξένος ;
sections.translation: My child , has the stranger left us ?

sections.label: Section 82
sections.original: βέβηκεν , ὥστε πᾶν ἐν ἡσύχῳ , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: He is gone , and so you can speak what you wish , father , fully at ease , knowing that I alone am near .

sections.label: Section 84
sections.original: ὦ πότνιαι δεινῶπες , εὖτε νῦν ἕδρας
sections.translation: Ladies of dread aspect , since your seat is

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: πρώτων ἐφʼ ὑμῶν τῆσδε γῆς ἔκαμψʼ ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: the first in this land at which I have bent my knee , show yourselves not ungracious to Phoebus or to myself ; who , when he proclaimed that doom of many woes , spoke to me of this rest after long years : on reaching my goal in a land where I should find a seat of the Awful Goddesses

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: σεμνῶν ἕδραν λάβοιμι καὶ ξενόστασιν ,
sections.translation: and a shelter for foreigners , there I should close my weary life , with profit , through my having fixed my abode there , for those who received me , but ruin for those who sent me forth , who drove me away . And he went on to warn me that signs of these things would come ,

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: ἢ σεισμὸν ἢ βροντήν τινʼ ἢ Διὸς σέλας ,
sections.translation:
in earthquake , or in thunder , or in the lightning of Zeus .
Now I perceive that in this journey some trusty omen from you has surely led me home to this grove ; never otherwise could I have met with you , first of all , in my wanderings — I , in my sobriety , with you who touch no wine ,

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: νήφων ἀοίνοις , κἀπὶ σεμνὸν ἑζόμην
sections.translation:
— or taken this august seat not shaped by men .
Then , goddesses , according to the word of Apollo , give me at last some way to accomplish and close my course — unless , perhaps , I seem too lowly ,

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: μόχθοις λατρεύων τοῖς ὑπερτάτοις βροτῶν .
sections.translation: enslaved as I am evermore to woes the sorest on the earth . Hear , sweet daughters of primeval Darkness ! Hear , you that are called the city of great Pallas , Athens , given most honor of all cities ! Pity this poor ghost of the man Oedipus !

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: εἴδωλον · οὐ γὰρ δὴ τόδʼ ἀρχαῖον δέμας .
sections.translation: For in truth it is the former living body no more .

sections.label: Section 111
sections.original: σίγα · πορεύονται γὰρ οἵδε δή τινες
sections.translation: Hush ! Here come some aged men to spy out your resting-place .

sections.label: Section 113
sections.original: σιγήσομαί τε καὶ σύ μʼ ἐξ ὁδοῦ πόδα
sections.translation: I will be mute . But hide me in the grove , apart from the road , till I learn
